questions.prompt: Difference between app_metadata and user_metadata in Supabase Auth?

choices.text: app_metadata is server-controlled — only Supabase or trusted backend code can write to it. Use for roles, subscription tier, admin flags, internal status — anything that affects authorization. user_metadata is user-controlled — users can update via signup or updateUser. Use for display preferences and profile fields the user owns. Critical rule: NEVER put authorization-affecting data in user_metadata; the user can change it and escalate privileges. Roles always go in app_metadata.

questions.explanation: app_metadata = server-controlled for authz. user_metadata = user-controlled for profile.

questions.prompt: Server-side route needs to know who's logged in. Use getSession() or getUser()?

choices.text: getUser() — it verifies the JWT against the auth server, returning a trusted user. getSession() reads the locally-stored session without verification; fine for client-side UI state (am I logged in?) but insufficient for server-side authorization decisions because a forged or tampered local session would pass. For SSR and API routes, always getUser(). The trade-off is one network call's latency for trusted identity.

questions.explanation: Server-side: getUser() (verified). Client-side UI: getSession() (fast, local).
